summary: Joe Reis announces his new 'Practical Data Modeling' project and upcoming book while sharing his 2024 goals including a data engineering course with deeplearning.ai. The newsletter curates weekend reads covering AI hype cycles, the failure modes of AI startups, and cultural commentary on topics from tipping to the potential return of a 'weird' decentralized internet.
key_points: New 'Practical Data Modeling' project launched with early book chapters
2024 will test whether AI delivers real value after 2023's hype cycle
AI startups still need fundamentals: product-market fit, paying customers, solving real problems
Chatbots may be solving UX problems that better search and design could address
money_quotes: 2023 was about the hype of AI. 2024 will be about where AI is a useful value-add.

Just because you're doing AI doesn't mean you're exempt from the age-old problems startups must overcome - solving legitimate business problems, a great customer experience, product-market fit, and getting paying customers.
LLM powered chatbots are cute, and I don't find them very a massively transformative technology.
The real value in a chat popup, in my view, is the live agent. They can do things and access info not readily available to users.
key_insight: Despite the AI hype, 2024 will be a reality check year where AI tools must prove actual business value rather than ride on technological novelty.
